_comment: Collision/seat semantics for the Office_Design_2 decomposition (ids = decomposed template ids). Base: all solid; the floor-palette carve opens floor-colored cells; walkableRects force-open lanes the dense art hides (chair bottoms/shadows overhang them); solid objects + solidRects repaint furniture and wall bands; seatObjects carve back walkable and become workSeats. Geometry from the exact decomposed bboxes: row-1 desk band x84-386 y72-162, row-2 band x52-450 y202-290, divider wall y296-352 with the single doorway strip x320-352 descending to the open bottom band y400-500 shared by break room and manager office.
_seat_note: obj-110 (break-room chair) is sealed off by the art (sub-16px gaps: cooler/fridge/plant/case chain) — decorative, not a workSeat.
_floor_patch_note: obj-92/93 are flat floor-texture pieces; on the object layer their anchors hide character bodies, so they render as boosted floor decals.
_clear_note: source-px zones where the gif used art that no pack ships: matched fragments there read as junk, so objects and substitute tiles inside are dropped (hole-fill lays the local wall/floor tile instead) and manualPlacements provide a clean native furnishing. Zones: the wall shelf (no modern twin; office mesh shelf stands in) and the left exterior wall (plain white in the design; drift-tolerant matching glued window-rung lookalikes onto it).
_wall_band_note: x0,y0,x1,y1,anchor — structure tiles inside a band y-sort at the band's floor line so characters north of the wall are occluded; mounted decor lifts just above. Doorway x316-352 excluded.
_fill_note: hole-fill donor override per zone (source px + tile id): cleared zones fill with the right wall tile instead of the local mode donor
